raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.3,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.5":"'Clean this up' plausibly includes improving the narrative arc and paragraph sequencing (e.g., the abrupt jump from optimization setup to the Lyapunov proof, or the final positioning sentence sitting detached at the end), making structural reorganization a candidate load-bearing dimension.","R1.6":"'Clean this up' most directly and naturally targets sentence- and paragraph-level prose quality — redundant phrasing, weak transitions, passive constructions — making writing polish the primary load-bearing interpretation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'clean this up' be confined to prose-level edits (sentence clarity, word choice, transitions, grammar) or does it also include restructuring the order and organization of the content?"}

rationale.R1.5: 'Clean this up' plausibly includes improving the narrative arc and paragraph sequencing (e.g., the abrupt jump from optimization setup to the Lyapunov proof, or the final positioning sentence sitting detached at the end), making structural reorganization a candidate load-bearing dimension.
rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' most directly and naturally targets sentence- and paragraph-level prose quality — redundant phrasing, weak transitions, passive constructions — making writing polish the primary load-bearing interpretation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'clean this up' be confined to prose-level edits (sentence clarity, word choice, transitions, grammar) or does it also include restructuring the order and organization of the content?
